rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CONNELLY VS. COLLEEN HOLLAND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 31, 2014, Line 10, Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order. Granted. Pursuant to CCP sec. 708.510(a)(2), Holland is entitled to an assignment of rights as to the rents that Connelly receives from his subtenant Katya Leu. Holland submits evidence that Connelly is the leaseholder of the apartment located at 3620-1/2 Wawona St. in San Francisco and that Connelly collects $2,100 per month from Ms. Leu. (Declaration of David Cook, Ex. B., Debtor Exam Transcript, p. 87: 7-25.) Connelly presents no evidence to support his contention that Ms. Leu pays her rent directly to the landlord. The Court is also inclined to grant the restraining order and turnover order; however, the proposed order as written is too broad. Holland shall submit a proposed order that clearly states that the restraining order and turnover order pertains to the rents that Connelly collects from Ms. Leu. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CONNELLY VS. COLLEEN HOLLAND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Authorizing Receiver To Sell Assets Of Receivership Estate And Approving Sale And Overbid Procedures
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 31, 2014, Line 10, OTHER SUSAN UECKER'S Motion For Order Authorizing Receiver To Sell Assets Of Receivership Estate And Approving Sale And Overbid Procedures. Denied without prejudice, to raising the issue of the sale of the business to the trial judge. At the trial, set to commence on January 5, 2015, the finder of fact will determine whether partnership dissolution is proper. Cross-Complainant Colleen Holland has not adjudicated this issue and the Law and Motion department declines to do so three court days before trial. The Court is not persuaded that if the sale of the business does not commence within the three days before trial, the Receiver will be unable to secure a buyer. The business has ceased to operate since October 2014 and there has been an insufficient showing that waiting a few more weeks will cause the business to lose all of its value. The interests of justice will be best served by having all of the outstanding issues pertaining to the business resolved at trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RAE LAIRD VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, December 31, 2014, Line 2: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Motion to Compel Discovery Responses and for Sanctions is GRANTED. Plaintiff RAE LAIRD is ORDERED to serve complete, verified responses without objection to Defendant's Special Interrogatories and Form Interrogatories served on July 2, 2014 within 15 days of service of the Notice of Entry of Order on this motion. Plaintiff RAE LAIRD is ORDERED to provide all responsive documents within his or her possession, custody and control to Defendant's Request for Production of Documents served on July 2, 2014 within 15 days of service of the Notice of Entry of Order on this motion. Plaintiff RAE LAIRD is ORDERED to pay $500.00 in sanctions by certified check made payable to the City and County of San Francisco within 15 days of service of the Notice of Entry of Order on this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt
